dialogueChunks.intro: The partner settlement received the stolen plans and the full accounting of who betrayed them. Their council sent a one-word reply: 'Begin.'
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} traveled to {location} personally to negotiate new terms. The old alliance is dead, but something more honest is growing in its place. No more backroom deals, no more secret negotiations. Every term is public, every obligation mutual. {quest_ally} says it's slower and harder than the old way. Also more durable.
dialogueChunks.outro: Trust built on truth survives what trust built on convenience cannot, {player_name}. You pulled the rot out by the root. What grows back will be stronger because of it.

playerResponses.text: An honest alliance is worth ten convenient ones.
Keep the traitors away from the negotiating table this time.

dialogueChunks.intro: The council confronted the captain with the evidence. She didn't deny it. Just took off her badge and set it on the table.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: She said the {enemy_type} threatened her family. That the first deal was survival and every deal after was too late to stop. {target_npc} doesn't know whether to believe her. Some of the guards she betrayed certainly don't. {quest_ally} has taken interim command while the garrison rebuilds trust in its own chain.
dialogueChunks.outro: The patrols are safe again, {player_name}. Whether the captain was a victim or a villain, the oath she broke put lives at risk. {quest_ally} says the new procedures require dual authorization for patrol schedules. No single point of failure, no single point of betrayal.

playerResponses.text: Betrayal under duress is still betrayal. But I hope she finds peace.
She made her choice. The garrison comes first.
Fix the system so one person can never compromise it again.

dialogueChunks.intro: The seal is back. The original charter is authenticated. The eviction notices are null and void. Twelve families are staying in their homes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The clerk was found at {location}, trying to sell the seal to a merchant who didn't ask questions. {target_npc} has them in custody. The founding family that benefited from the forgery is claiming ignorance, but the evidence {quest_ally} compiled suggests otherwise. The regional authority is reviewing every document the clerk ever touched.
dialogueChunks.outro: A community's word is only as good as the people who keep the records, {player_name}. You made sure the truth was louder than the lie. Those families will remember that when the next crisis tests what this settlement is made of.

playerResponses.text: The pen can be as dangerous as the sword. Guard your records well.
Hold the founding family accountable too. Ignorance is a convenient excuse.
